doctrine_enforcement_lines: Implementation is not stale by itself. Tickets become stale.
Apply maximum concrete capability within the approved scope of each ticket.
coverage_notes: source authority, contracts, data schema, approved api boundary, runtime service, validation, evidence, gate, dependency order, runtime validation, and screen contract applicability are represented in structured ticket fields.
future_refresh_policy: Future draft requires repo refresh, staleness scan, dependency refresh, ticket-pack re-audit, scale_decomposition_audit, predictive stop analysis, autonomous readiness, and explicit human approval before execution.
execution_lock: execution_locked before execution until refresh and re-audit are complete

tickets.title: T2-L5-001 configuration schema registry
tickets.objective: Define the configuration schema registry implementation surface for Train 2 Level 5 with source authority, contracts, data schema references, approved api boundary, validation ladder, and evidence artifact. Future draft refresh, re-audit, and execution_locked before execution are mandatory before this ticket may be considered for readiness.
tickets.scope: configuration schema registry owns one bounded level_5 surface. Include source authority, contract references, data schema references, approved api action authority, runtime service checks when applicable, dependency order, evidence path, and frontend_or_screen_contract_when_applicable = not_applicable_for_this_train_except_future_screen_contract_references.
tickets.non_scope: No production deployment activation, protected value access, module frontend implementation outside approved screen contracts, predictive stop analysis, autonomous readiness, execution prompt creation, ticket execution, runtime code edit by this planning task, Prisma migration edit by this planning task, package change, or Spark Genesis repository change.
required_outputs: configuration schema registry implementation result or decision artifact
tests_required: contract or behavior test tied to the ticket surface
negative boundary test where the surface handles permission or tenant isolation
health smoke verification when runtime service or api boundary is touched
validation_commands: local startup health smoke verification for the affected service surface after exact-file planning
stop_conditions: Stop if exact-file plan reveals work requires files_forbidden_to_change.
Stop if the ticket cannot be completed without making an architectural decision not present in any committed source document.
Stop if validation fails after 3 bounded repair attempts.
Stop if work drifts into runtime / Foundry / module installer / business modules / production auth / deployment / secrets / destructive migrations without explicit ticket authority.
Stop if a Prisma schema migration is required but not authorized in files_expected_to_change.
acceptance_criteria: configuration schema registry has a bounded source-authorized exact-file plan before work starts.
CI command result and named evidence artifact prove the minimum concrete requirement.
Approved api action authority, data schema reference, contract reference, evidence artifact, validation ladder, and dependency order are recorded.
Screen contract applicability is explicitly resolved before any future UI work.
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert the future commit and preserve codex_review/core/v0_0_2/full_train_preplanning_v2/t2-l5-001_evidence.md as failed-run evidence if validation cannot be repaired within bounded attempts.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Required if exact-file planning finds stale source authority, missing dependency, train-boundary leakage, or a file outside approved scope.
tickets.stale_ticket_risk_notes: Ticket becomes stale if source paths, lower-train dependency state, contract authority, data schema authority, validation command behavior, or evidence path changes before execution. Future draft refresh, re-audit, and execution_locked before execution are mandatory before this ticket may be considered for readiness.
tickets.runtime_consistency_chain: source authority -> exact-file plan -> implementation surface -> contract/data/api validation -> health smoke verification -> evidence artifact -> gate closure
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: configuration schema registry produces an observable behavior, CI command result, named evidence artifact, negative test, or measured target tied to the approved source authority.
tickets.split_if: Split only if exact-file planning discovers distinct architectural decisions, unrelated validation ladders, separate runtime subsystems, or overlapping ownership.

tickets.runtime_consistency_chain: source authority -> exact-file plan -> implementation surface -> contract/data/api validation -> health smoke verification -> evidence artifact -> gate closure
tickets.minimum_concrete_requirement: Level 5 validation gate produces an observable behavior, CI command result, named evidence artifact, negative test, or measured target tied to the approved source authority.
tickets.split_if: Split only if exact-file planning discovers distinct architectural decisions, unrelated validation ladders, separate runtime subsystems, or overlapping ownership.

tickets.title: T2-G999 Train 2 Level 5 final gate
tickets.objective: Define the Train 2 Level 5 final gate implementation surface for Train 2 Level 5 with source authority, contracts, data schema references, approved api boundary, validation ladder, and evidence artifact. Future draft refresh, re-audit, and execution_locked before execution are mandatory before this ticket may be considered for readiness.
tickets.scope: Train 2 Level 5 final gate owns one bounded level_5 surface. Include source authority, contract references, data schema references, approved api action authority, runtime service checks when applicable, dependency order, evidence path, and frontend_or_screen_contract_when_applicable = not_applicable_for_this_train_except_future_screen_contract_references.
tickets.non_scope: No production deployment activation, protected value access, module frontend implementation outside approved screen contracts, predictive stop analysis, autonomous readiness, execution prompt creation, ticket execution, runtime code edit by this planning task, Prisma migration edit by this planning task, package change, or Spark Genesis repository change.
required_outputs: Train 2 Level 5 final gate exact-file plan
Train 2 Level 5 final gate implementation result or decision artifact
Train 2 Level 5 final gate validation result
Train 2 Level 5 final gate evidence artifact
tests_required: contract or behavior test tied to the ticket surface
negative boundary test where the surface handles permission or tenant isolation
health smoke verification when runtime service or api boundary is touched
validation_commands: local startup health smoke verification for the affected service surface after exact-file planning
stop_conditions: Stop if exact-file plan reveals work requires files_forbidden_to_change.
Stop if the ticket cannot be completed without making an architectural decision not present in any committed source document.
Stop if validation fails after 3 bounded repair attempts.
Stop if work drifts into runtime / Foundry / module installer / business modules / production auth / deployment / secrets / destructive migrations without explicit ticket authority.
Stop if a Prisma schema migration is required but not authorized in files_expected_to_change.
acceptance_criteria: Train 2 Level 5 final gate has a bounded source-authorized exact-file plan before work starts.
CI command result and named evidence artifact prove the minimum concrete requirement.
Approved api action authority, data schema reference, contract reference, evidence artifact, validation ladder, and dependency order are recorded.
Screen contract applicability is explicitly resolved before any future UI work.
tickets.commit_message: feat: deliver train 2 level 5 final gate
tickets.rollback_notes: Revert the future commit and preserve codex_review/core/v0_0_2/full_train_preplanning_v2/t2-g999_evidence.md as failed-run evidence if validation cannot be repaired within bounded attempts.
tickets.conditional_replan_required: Required if exact-file planning finds stale source authority, missing dependency, train-boundary leakage, or a file outside approved scope.
